implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-006 (DDR-003 four pragmas on both helpers)
Producer side of PENDING_APPROVAL_REQUEST_ID integration contract for TASK-PSM-007
Producer side of SCHEMA_INITIALIZED contract for TASK-PSM-005/006/007

completion_promises.criterion_text: schema.sql contains CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS builds (...) STRICT with all columns from API-sqlite-schema.md §2.1 PLUS the new pending_approval_request_id TEXT column
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/lifecycle/schema.sql §2.1 reproduces every column from API-sqlite-schema.md §2.1 (build_id, feature_id, repo, branch, feature_yaml_path, project, status, triggered_by, originating_adapter, originating_user, correlation_id, parent_request_id, queued_at, started_at, completed_at, worktree_path, pr_url, error, max_turns, sdk_timeout_seconds) and adds the F4 recovery column `pending_approval_request_id TEXT`. The CREATE TABLE statement ends with the STRICT marker. test_schema_defines_strict_builds_table_with_pending_approval_column asserts presence of both the STRICT marker and the new column.

completion_promises.criterion_text: schema.sql contains CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stage_log (...) STRICT with all columns and indices from API-sqlite-schema.md §2.2
completion_promises.evidence: schema.sql §2.2 declares stage_log STRICT with id (autoincrement), build_id FK, stage_label, target_kind+CHECK, target_identifier, status+CHECK, gate_mode+CHECK, coach_score, threshold_applied, started_at, completed_at, duration_secs, details_json — plus idx_stage_log_build and the partial idx_stage_log_gated index per API contract §2.2. Verified by test_schema_defines_stage_log_with_indices.

completion_promises.criterion_text: schema.sql contains CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version with a seed row INSERT OR IGNORE INTO schema_version VALUES (1, datetime('now'))
completion_promises.evidence: schema.sql §2.3 declares the schema_version STRICT table and follows it with `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO schema_version (version, applied_at) VALUES (1, datetime('now'));`. test_schema_defines_schema_version_with_seed_row and test_apply_at_boot_creates_tables_and_seeds_schema_version both verify presence and runtime behaviour.

completion_promises.criterion_text: schema.sql contains the UNIQUE INDEX on (feature_id, correlation_id)
completion_promises.evidence: schema.sql defines `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uq_builds_feature_correlation ON builds (feature_id, correlation_id);`. test_schema_declares_unique_feature_correlation_index asserts the index name and UNIQUE marker; test_unique_feature_correlation_index_rejects_duplicates verifies enforcement at runtime by inserting a duplicate (feature_id, correlation_id) pair and asserting sqlite3.IntegrityError.

completion_promises.criterion_text: migrations.apply_at_boot(connection) is idempotent (running twice is a no-op) and verifiable
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/lifecycle/migrations.py implements apply_at_boot which (1) reads MAX(version) from schema_version, defaulting to 0 when the table does not yet exist, (2) skips already-applied migrations, and (3) wraps executescript in a `with connection:` transaction so failures roll back. The bundled schema.sql is itself idempotent (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + INSERT OR IGNORE). test_apply_at_boot_is_idempotent calls apply_at_boot twice and asserts schema_version still contains exactly one row [(1,)].

completion_promises.criterion_text: Both connection helpers apply the four mandatory pragmas (per DDR-003): journal_mode = WAL, synchronous = NORMAL, foreign_keys = ON, busy_timeout = 5000
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/adapters/sqlite/connect.py defines _apply_pragmas() called from both connect_writer and read_only_connect. It sets journal_mode=WAL on the writer (read-only connections inherit WAL state and refuse the mutating form), and unconditionally sets synchronous=NORMAL, foreign_keys=ON, busy_timeout=5000 on every open. test_connect_writer_applies_four_mandatory_pragmas reads back all four pragmas via PRAGMA round-trip and asserts the expected values (wal, 1, 1, 5000).

completion_promises.criterion_text: read_only_connect() opens with mode=ro URI (sqlite3.connect(f"file:{path}?mode=ro", uri=True))
completion_promises.evidence: read_only_connect builds `uri = f"file:{db_path}?mode=ro"` and calls `sqlite3.connect(uri, uri=True, ...)`. test_read_only_connect_uses_mode_ro_uri_and_rejects_writes seeds a row via connect_writer, then opens via read_only_connect, reads the row back successfully, and asserts that an UPDATE raises sqlite3.OperationalError — proving SQLite engaged the read-only mode rather than just returning a plain connection.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Integration test: open fresh DB, apply migrations, assert PRAGMA journal_mode == "wal" and SELECT version FROM schema_version returns 1
completion_promises.evidence: test_apply_migrations_then_journal_mode_is_wal opens a fresh DB in tmp_path via connect_writer, runs migrations.apply_at_boot, asserts PRAGMA journal_mode == 'wal' and SELECT version FROM schema_version == (1,). Passes (see Phase 4.5 transcript).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Integration test: STRICT enforcement — inserting a row with a non-INTEGER max_turns raises sqlite3.IntegrityError
completion_promises.evidence: test_strict_table_rejects_non_integer_max_turns inserts a builds row with max_turns='not-an-int' and expects sqlite3.IntegrityError. The STRICT marker on the builds table is what causes SQLite to reject the value rather than silently coercing it. Passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Integration test: WAL files (forge.db-wal, forge.db-shm) are created alongside the main DB after first write
completion_promises.evidence: test_wal_sidecar_files_appear_after_first_write opens connect_writer, applies migrations, executes an INSERT + commit, and (while the connection is still open, before SQLite's close-time checkpoint can collapse the WAL) asserts forge.db, forge.db-wal, and forge.db-shm all exist on disk. Passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: All new source files use type hints, snake_case names, PEP 8 line-lengths consistent with the existing forge.adapters.nats modules (88-char Black target), `from __future__ import annotations`, module-level docstrings, and structured Google-style docstrings on every public symbol. No project-level pre-commit/ruff/black config is wired into pyproject.toml in this worktree (only pytest config), so 'project-configured' lint amounts to the style guide in .claude/rules/code-style.md, which is satisfied. The pytest run completes with zero collection-time warnings.
